constraints: Aarsi and Krypto were competing in a shooting game.
You are given a matrix AAA of size N×MN \times MN×M representing a shooting board. The cells of the matrix contain information about which player took a shot at which cell:
If A(i,j)=0A_{(i,j)} = 0A(i,j)​=0, no player took a shot at that cell.
If A(i,j)=1A_{(i,j)} = 1A(i,j)​=1, Aarsi took a shot at that cell.
If A(i,j)=2A_{(i,j)} = 2A(i,j)​=2, Krypto took a shot at that cell.
If A(i,j)=3A_{(i,j)} = 3A(i,j)​=3, both players took a shot at that cell.

Let the bullseye cell be (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1​,y1​), then, for a shot at cell (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2​,y2​), the score of the shot is calculated as max⁡(∣x1−x2∣,∣y1−y2∣)\max(|x_1 - x_2|, |y_1 - y_2|)max(∣x1​−x2​∣,∣y1​−y2​∣). The score of a player is the sum of scores of all shots by that player.
Your task is to consider each cell in the matrix as the bullseye cell and find the value of ∣|∣Aarsi's score - Krypto's score∣|∣ accordingly.
The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of rows and columns in the matrix, respectively.

The next NNN lines describe the matrix AAA. The ithi^{th}ith of these lines contain MMM space-separated integers ― the values A(i,1),A(i,2),…,A(i,M)A_{(i, 1)}, A_{(i, 2)}, \ldots, A_{(i, M)}A(i,1)​,A(i,2)​,…,A(i,M)​.
For each test case, output a matrix BBB where B(i,j)B{(i, j)}B(i,j) denotes the value of ∣|∣Aarsi's score - Krypto's score∣|∣ considering (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) as the bullseye cell.
The sum of N⋅MN\cdot MN⋅M over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.
